steps.title: Identify the grammatical structure and subjects
steps.content: The sentence connects two independent clauses with “and”: “DL pertains to...” and “TML _____ to...”. Both DL and TML are singular subjects.

steps.title: Apply the subject-verb agreement rule
steps.content: ”Pertains” (B) is the only singular verb form, maintaining parallel structure with the first clause and proper agreement with TML. The other options—”are,” “have,” and “pertain”—are plural or base-form verbs. That matches choice B — B is correct.
